509: func (c *Command) ParseFlagsAndConfig(args []string) int {
510: 	var err error
511: 
512: 	f := c.Flags()
513: 
514: 	if err = f.Parse(args); err != nil {
515: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
516: 		return base.CommandUserError
517: 	}
518: 
519: 	if len(c.flagConfig) == 0 && c.presetConfig == nil {
520: 		c.UI.Error("Must specify a config file using -config")
521: 		return base.CommandUserError
522: 	}
523: 
524: 	cfg, out := c.reloadConfig()
525: 	if out > 0 {
526: 		return out
527: 	}
528: 	c.Config = cfg
529: 
530: 	if c.Config.Controller == nil && c.Config.Worker == nil {
531: 		c.UI.Error("Neither worker nor controller specified in configuration file.")
532: 		return base.CommandUserError
533: 	}
534: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil && c.Config.Controller.Name == "" {
535: 		c.UI.Error("Controller has no name set. It must be the unique name of this instance.")
536: 		return base.CommandUserError
537: 	}
538: 	if c.Config.Worker != nil && c.Config.Worker.Name == "" {
539: 		c.UI.Error("Worker has no name set. It must be the unique name of this instance.")
540: 		return base.CommandUserError
541: 	}
542: 
543: 	return base.CommandSuccess
544: }

663: func (c *Command) WaitForInterrupt() int {
664: 	const op = "server.(Command).WaitForInterrupt"
665: 	// Wait for shutdown
666: 	shutdownTriggered := false
667: 
668: 	for !shutdownTriggered {
669: 		select {
670: 		case <-c.ShutdownCh:
671: 			c.UI.Output("==> Boundary server shutdown triggered, interrupt again to force")
672: 
673: 			// Add a force-shutdown goroutine to consume another interrupt
674: 			abortForceShutdownCh := make(chan struct{})
675: 			defer close(abortForceShutdownCh)
676: 			go func() {
677: 				shutdownCh := make(chan os.Signal, 4)
678: 				signal.Notify(shutdownCh, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
679: 				select {
680: 				case <-shutdownCh:
681: 					c.UI.Error("Second interrupt received, forcing shutdown")
682: 					os.Exit(base.CommandUserError)
683: 				case <-abortForceShutdownCh:
684: 					// No-op, we just use this to shut down the goroutine
685: 				}
686: 			}()
687: 
688: 			if c.Config.Controller != nil {
689: 				c.opsServer.WaitIfHealthExists(c.Config.Controller.GracefulShutdownWaitDuration, c.UI)
690: 			}
691: 
692: 			// Do worker shutdown
693: 			if c.Config.Worker != nil {
694: 				if err := c.worker.Shutdown(); err != nil {
695: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down worker: %w", err).Error())
696: 				}
697: 			}
698: 
699: 			// Do controller shutdown
700: 			if c.Config.Controller != nil {
701: 				if err := c.controller.Shutdown(); err != nil {
702: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down controller: %w", err).Error())
703: 				}
704: 			}
705: 
706: 			err := c.opsServer.Shutdown()
707: 			if err != nil {
708: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down ops listeners: %w", err).Error())
709: 			}
710: 
711: 			shutdownTriggered = true
712: 
713: 		case <-c.SighupCh:
714: 			c.UI.Output("==> Boundary server reload triggered")
715: 
716: 			// Check for new log level
717: 			var level hclog.Level
718: 			var newConf *config.Config
719: 			var out int
720: 
721: 			if c.flagConfig == "" && c.presetConfig == nil {
722: 				goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
723: 			}
724: 
725: 			newConf, out = c.reloadConfig()
726: 			if out > 0 {
727: 				goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
728: 			}
729: 
730: 			// Ensure at least one config was found.
731: 			if newConf == nil {
732: 				event.WriteError(context.TODO(), op, stderrors.New("no config found at reload time"))
733: 				goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
734: 			}
735: 
736: 			if newConf.LogLevel != "" {
737: 				configLogLevel := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(newConf.LogLevel))
738: 				switch configLogLevel {
739: 				case "trace":
740: 					level = hclog.Trace
741: 				case "debug":
742: 					level = hclog.Debug
743: 				case "notice", "info", "":
744: 					level = hclog.Info
745: 				case "warn", "warning":
746: 					level = hclog.Warn
747: 				case "err", "error":
748: 					level = hclog.Error
749: 				default:
750: 					event.WriteError(context.TODO(), op, stderrors.New("unknown log level found on reload"), event.WithInfo("level", newConf.LogLevel))
751: 					goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
752: 				}
753: 				c.Logger.SetLevel(level)
754: 			}
755: 
756: 		RUNRELOADFUNCS:
757: 			if err := c.Reload(newConf); err != nil {
758: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error(s) were encountered during reload: %w", err).Error())
759: 			}
760: 
761: 		case <-c.SigUSR2Ch:
762: 			buf := make([]byte, 32*1024*1024)
763: 			n := runtime.Stack(buf[:], true)
764: 			event.WriteSysEvent(context.TODO(), op, "goroutine trace", "stack", string(buf[:n]))
765: 		}
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	return base.CommandSuccess
769: }

771: func (c *Command) Reload(newConf *config.Config) error {
772: 	c.ReloadFuncsLock.RLock()
773: 	defer c.ReloadFuncsLock.RUnlock()
774: 
775: 	var reloadErrors *multierror.Error
776: 
777: 	for _, relFunc := range c.ReloadFuncs["listeners"] {
778: 		if relFunc != nil {
779: 			if err := relFunc(); err != nil {
780: 				reloadErrors = multierror.Append(reloadErrors, fmt.Errorf("error encountered reloading listener: %w", err))
781: 			}
782: 		}
783: 	}
784: 
785: 	if newConf != nil && c.worker != nil {
786: 		c.worker.ParseAndStoreTags(newConf.Worker.Tags)
787: 	}
788: 
789: 	// Send a message that we reloaded. This prevents "guessing" sleep times
790: 	// in tests.
791: 	if c.reloadedCh != nil {
792: 		select {
793: 		case c.reloadedCh <- struct{}{}:
794: 		default:
795: 		}
796: 	}
797: 
798: 	return reloadErrors.ErrorOrNil()
799: }

60: // apiHandler returns an http.Handler for the services. This can be used on
61: // its own to mount the Controller API within another web server.
62: func (c *Controller) apiHandler(props HandlerProperties) (http.Handler, error) {
63: 	mux := http.NewServeMux()
64: 
65: 	grpcGwMux := newGrpcGatewayMux()
66: 	err := registerGrpcGatewayEndpoints(props.CancelCtx, grpcGwMux, gatewayDialOptions(c.apiGrpcServerListener)...)
67: 	if err != nil {
68: 		return nil, err
69: 	}
70: 	mux.Handle("/v1/nodes", handleNodes(c))
71: 	mux.Handle("/v1/", grpcGwMux)
72: 	mux.Handle("/", handleUi(c))
73: 
74: 	corsWrappedHandler := wrapHandlerWithCors(mux, props)
75: 	commonWrappedHandler := wrapHandlerWithCommonFuncs(corsWrappedHandler, c, props)
76: 	callbackInterceptingHandler := wrapHandlerWithCallbackInterceptor(commonWrappedHandler, c)
77: 	printablePathCheckHandler := cleanhttp.PrintablePathCheckHandler(callbackInterceptingHandler, nil)
78: 	eventsHandler, err := common.WrapWithEventsHandler(printablePathCheckHandler, c.conf.Eventer, c.kms, props.ListenerConfig)
79: 	if err != nil {
80: 		return nil, err
81: 	}
82: 	metricsHandler := metric.InstrumentApiHandler(eventsHandler)
83: 
84: 	return metricsHandler, nil
85: }

87: // GetHealthHandler returns a gRPC Gateway mux that is registered against the
88: // controller's gRPC health service to make it accessible from an HTTP API.
89: func (c *Controller) GetHealthHandler(lcfg *listenerutil.ListenerConfig) (http.Handler, error) {
90: 	const op = "controller.(Controller).GetHealthHandler"
91: 	if lcfg == nil {
92: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: received nil listener config", op)
93: 	}
94: 
95: 	healthGrpcGwMux := newGrpcGatewayMux()
96: 	err := registerHealthGrpcGatewayEndpoint(c.baseContext, healthGrpcGwMux, gatewayDialOptions(c.apiGrpcServerListener)...)
97: 	if err != nil {
98: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: failed to register health service handler: %w", op, err)
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	wrapped := wrapHandlerWithCommonFuncs(healthGrpcGwMux, c, HandlerProperties{lcfg, c.baseContext})
102: 	return common.WrapWithEventsHandler(wrapped, c.conf.Eventer, c.kms, lcfg)
103: }

299: func wrapHandlerWithCommonFuncs(h http.Handler, c *Controller, props HandlerProperties) http.Handler {
300: 	const op = "controller.wrapHandlerWithCommonFuncs"
301: 	var maxRequestDuration time.Duration
302: 	var maxRequestSize int64
303: 	if props.ListenerConfig != nil {
304: 		maxRequestDuration = props.ListenerConfig.MaxRequestDuration
305: 		maxRequestSize = props.ListenerConfig.MaxRequestSize
306: 	}
307: 	if maxRequestDuration == 0 {
308: 		maxRequestDuration = globals.DefaultMaxRequestDuration
309: 	}
310: 	if maxRequestSize == 0 {
311: 		maxRequestSize = globals.DefaultMaxRequestSize
312: 	}
313: 
314: 	disableAuthzFailures := c.conf.DisableAuthorizationFailures ||
315: 		(c.conf.RawConfig.DevController && os.Getenv("BOUNDARY_DEV_SKIP_AUTHZ") != "")
316: 	if disableAuthzFailures {
317: 		event.WriteSysEvent(context.TODO(), op, "AUTHORIZATION CHECKING DISABLED")
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
321: 		// Set the Cache-Control header for all responses returned
322: 		w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-store")
323: 
324: 		// Start with the request context and our timeout
325: 		ctx, cancelFunc := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), maxRequestDuration)
326: 		defer cancelFunc()
327: 
328: 		// Add a size limiter if desired
329: 		if maxRequestSize > 0 {
330: 			ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, globals.ContextMaxRequestSizeTypeKey, maxRequestSize)
331: 		}
332: 
333: 		// Add values for authn/authz checking
334: 		requestInfo := authpb.RequestInfo{
335: 			Path:                 r.URL.Path,
336: 			Method:               r.Method,
337: 			DisableAuthzFailures: disableAuthzFailures,
338: 		}
339: 
340: 		requestInfo.PublicId, requestInfo.EncryptedToken, requestInfo.TokenFormat = auth.GetTokenFromRequest(ctx, c.kms, r)
341: 
342: 		if info, ok := event.RequestInfoFromContext(ctx); ok {
343: 			// piggyback some eventing fields with the auth info proto message
344: 			requestInfo.EventId = info.EventId
345: 			requestInfo.TraceId = info.Id
346: 			requestInfo.ClientIp = info.ClientIp
347: 		} else {
348: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
349: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, errors.New("unable to read event request info from context"))
350: 			return
351: 		}
352: 
353: 		// Serialize the request info to send it across the wire to the
354: 		// grpc-gateway via an http header
355: 		requestInfo.Ticket = c.apiGrpcGatewayTicket // allows the grpc-gateway to verify the request info came from it's in-memory companion http proxy
356: 		marshalledRequestInfo, err := proto.Marshal(&requestInfo)
357: 		if err != nil {
358: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error marshaling request info"))
359: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
360: 			return
361: 		}
362: 		// Use the default grpc-gateway mapping rule to pass the request info as
363: 		// metadata.
364: 		// See: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/grpc-ecosystem/grpc-gateway/runtime#DefaultHeaderMatcher
365: 		r.Header.Set("Grpc-Metadata-"+requestInfoMdKey, base58.FastBase58Encoding(marshalledRequestInfo))
366: 
367: 		// Set the context back on the request
368: 		r = r.Clone(ctx)
369: 		h.ServeHTTP(w, r)
370: 	})
371: }

373: func wrapHandlerWithCors(h http.Handler, props HandlerProperties) http.Handler {
374: 	allowedMethods := []string{
375: 		http.MethodDelete,
376: 		http.MethodGet,
377: 		http.MethodOptions,
378: 		http.MethodPost,
379: 		http.MethodPatch,
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	allowedOrigins := props.ListenerConfig.CorsAllowedOrigins
383: 
384: 	allowedHeaders := append([]string{
385: 		"Content-Type",
386: 		"X-Requested-With",
387: 		"Authorization",
388: 	}, props.ListenerConfig.CorsAllowedHeaders...)
389: 
390: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
391: 		if props.ListenerConfig.CorsEnabled == nil || !*props.ListenerConfig.CorsEnabled {
392: 			h.ServeHTTP(w, req)
393: 			return
394: 		}
395: 
396: 		origin := req.Header.Get("Origin")
397: 
398: 		if origin == "" {
399: 			// Serve directly
400: 			h.ServeHTTP(w, req)
401: 			return
402: 		}
403: 
404: 		// Check origin
405: 		var valid bool
406: 		switch {
407: 		case len(allowedOrigins) == 0:
408: 			// not valid
409: 
410: 		case len(allowedOrigins) == 1 && allowedOrigins[0] == "*":
411: 			valid = true
412: 
413: 		default:
414: 			valid = strutil.StrListContains(allowedOrigins, origin)
415: 		}
416: 
417: 		if !valid {
418: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
419: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)
420: 
421: 			err := handlers.ApiErrorWithCodeAndMessage(codes.PermissionDenied, "origin forbidden")
422: 
423: 			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
424: 			_ = enc.Encode(err)
425: 			return
426: 		}
427: 
428: 		if req.Method == http.MethodOptions &&
429: 			!strutil.StrListContains(allowedMethods, req.Header.Get("Access-Control-Request-Method")) {
430: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
431: 			return
432: 		}
433: 
434: 		w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", origin)
435: 		w.Header().Set("Vary", "Origin")
436: 
437: 		// Apply headers for preflight requests
438: 		if req.Method == http.MethodOptions {
439: 			w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", strings.Join(allowedMethods, ", "))
440: 			w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", strings.Join(allowedHeaders, ", "))
441: 			w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Max-Age", "300")
442: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
443: 			return
444: 		}
445: 
446: 		h.ServeHTTP(w, req)
447: 	})
448: }

455: func wrapHandlerWithCallbackInterceptor(h http.Handler, c *Controller) http.Handler {
456: 	logCallbackErrors := os.Getenv("BOUNDARY_LOG_CALLBACK_ERRORS") != ""
457: 
458: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
459: 		const op = "controller.wrapHandlerWithCallbackInterceptor"
460: 		ctx := req.Context()
461: 		var err error
462: 		id, err := event.NewId(event.IdField)
463: 		if err != nil {
464: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
465: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("unable to create id for event", "method", req.Method, "url", req.URL.RequestURI()))
466: 			return
467: 		}
468: 		info := &event.RequestInfo{
469: 			EventId:  id,
470: 			Id:       common.GeneratedTraceId(ctx),
471: 			PublicId: "unknown",
472: 			Method:   req.Method,
473: 			Path:     req.URL.RequestURI(),
474: 		}
475: 		ctx, err = event.NewRequestInfoContext(ctx, info)
476: 		if err != nil {
477: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
478: 			event.WriteError(req.Context(), op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("unable to create context with request info", "method", req.Method, "url", req.URL.RequestURI()))
479: 			return
480: 		}
481: 		// If this doesn't have a callback suffix on a supported action, serve
482: 		// normally
483: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(req.URL.Path, ":authenticate:callback") {
484: 			h.ServeHTTP(w, req)
485: 			return
486: 		}
487: 
488: 		req.URL.Path = strings.TrimSuffix(req.URL.Path, ":callback")
489: 
490: 		// How we get the parameters changes based on the method. Right now only
491: 		// GET is supported with query args, but this can support POST with JSON
492: 		// or URL-encoded args. In those cases, the MIME type would have to be

494: 		// you'd use a json.RawMessage for Attributes consisting of the body. Or
495: 		// something very similar to that.
496: 		var useForm bool
497: 		switch req.Method {
498: 		case http.MethodGet:
499: 			if err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {
500: 				if logCallbackErrors && c != nil {
501: 					event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("callback error"))
502: 				}
503: 				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)
504: 				return
505: 			}
506: 			useForm = true
507: 		}
508: 
509: 		attrs := &cmdAttrs{
510: 			Command: "callback",
511: 		}
512: 
513: 		switch {
514: 		case useForm:
515: 			if len(req.Form) > 0 {
516: 				values := make(map[string]interface{}, len(req.Form))
517: 				// This won't handle repeated values. That's fine, at least for now.
518: 				// We can address that if needed, which seems unlikely.
519: 				for k := range req.Form {
520: 					values[k] = req.Form.Get(k)

523: 				if strings.HasSuffix(req.URL.Path, "oidc:authenticate") {
524: 					if s, ok := values["state"].(string); ok {

527: 							event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error marshaling state"))

529: 							return
530: 						}
531: 						if stateWrapper.AuthMethodId == "" {
532: 							event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("missing auth method id"))
533: 							w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
534: 							return
535: 						}
536: 						stripped := strings.TrimSuffix(req.URL.Path, "oidc:authenticate")

539: 						event.WriteError(ctx, op, errors.New("missing state parameter"))

541: 						return
542: 					}
543: 				}
544: 				attrs.Attributes = values
545: 			}

547: 			attrBytes, err := json.Marshal(attrs)

549: 				if logCallbackErrors && c != nil {
550: 					event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error marshaling json"))

554: 			}

556: 			// If there is any existing body, close it as we're going to replace
557: 			// it. It shouldn't be populated in this code path, but you never
558: 			// know.

564: 				}

567: 			req.Body = ioutil.NopCloser(bytesReader)

570: 			req.Method = http.MethodPost
571: 		}
572: 
573: 		h.ServeHTTP(w, req)
574: 	})

579: 		switch req.Method {
580: 
581: 		case http.MethodPost:
582: 			body, err := io.ReadAll(req.Body)

585: 				_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))

587: 				return

311: func (c *Controller) Start() error {

318: 	c.baseContext, c.baseCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())

326: 		return err
327: 	}

329: 	if err := c.registerJobs(); err != nil {

331: 	}
332: 	if err := c.startListeners(); err != nil {

334: 	}

336: 	// Upsert controller before starting tickers and scheduler to ensure the controller exists

339: 	}
340: 	if err := c.scheduler.Start(c.baseContext, c.schedulerWg); err != nil {
341: 		return fmt.Errorf("error starting scheduler: %w", err)
342: 	}
343: 
344: 	c.tickerWg.Add(5)

356: 	}()
357: 	go func() {
358: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()

360: 	}()
361: 	go func() {
362: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()

366: 	return nil

54: func (w *Worker) configureForWorker(ln *base.ServerListener, logger *log.Logger) (func(), error) {

56: 
57: 	handler, err := w.handler(HandlerProperties{ListenerConfig: ln.Config})

59: 		return nil, err

62: 	cancelCtx := w.baseContext

87: 	fetchCredsFn := func(

166: func (w *Worker) stopServersAndListeners() error {
167: 	var mg multierror.Group
168: 	mg.Go(w.stopHttpServer)
169: 	mg.Go(w.stopClusterGrpcServer)
170: 
171: 	// FIXME (jeff): For some reason, unlike the controller, the grpc server
172: 	// really likes to hang on closing. Maybe because it's never served a
173: 	// connection? This is a workaround to force it until I can dig in.
174: 	var cancel context.CancelFunc
175: 	if w.nodeeSplitListener != nil {
176: 		var ctx context.Context
177: 		ctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, 2*time.Second)
178: 		go func() {
179: 			<-ctx.Done()
180: 			w.nodeeSplitListener.Stop()
181: 			cancel()
182: 		}()
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	stopErrors := mg.Wait()
186: 
187: 	if w.nodeeSplitListener != nil {
188: 		cancel()
189: 		err := w.nodeeSplitListener.Stop()
190: 		if err != nil {
191: 			stopErrors = multierror.Append(stopErrors, err)
192: 		}
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	err := w.stopAnyListeners()
196: 	if err != nil {
197: 		stopErrors = multierror.Append(stopErrors, err)
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	return stopErrors.ErrorOrNil()
201: }

209: 		return nil
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, w.proxyListener.Config.MaxRequestDuration)
213: 	w.proxyListener.HTTPServer.Shutdown(ctx)
214: 	cancel()
215: 
216: 	return nil
217: }
218: 
219: func (w *Worker) stopClusterGrpcServer() error {
220: 	if w.proxyListener == nil {
221: 		return nil
222: 	}
223: 	if w.proxyListener.GrpcServer == nil {
224: 		return nil
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	w.proxyListener.GrpcServer.GracefulStop()
228: 	return nil
229: }
230: 
231: // stopAnyListeners does a final once over the known
232: // listeners to make sure we didn't miss any;
233: // expected to run at the end of stopServersAndListeners.
234: func (w *Worker) stopAnyListeners() error {
235: 	if w.proxyListener == nil {
236: 		return nil
237: 	}
238: 	var closeErrors *multierror.Error
239: 	var err error
240: 	if w.nodeeSplitListener != nil {
241: 		err = w.nodeeSplitListener.Stop()
242: 	} else if w.proxyListener.ProxyListener != nil {
243: 		err = w.proxyListener.ProxyListener.Close()
244: 	}
245: 	err = listenerCloseErrorCheck("proxy", err)
246: 	if err != nil {
247: 		closeErrors = multierror.Append(closeErrors, err)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	return closeErrors.ErrorOrNil()
251: }
252: 
253: // listenerCloseErrorCheck does some validation on an error returned
254: // by a net.Listener's Close function, and ignores a few cases
255: // where we don't actually want an error to be returned.
256: func listenerCloseErrorCheck(lnType string, err error) error {
257: 	if errors.Is(err, net.ErrClosed) {
258: 		// Ignore net.ErrClosed - The listener was already closed,
259: 		// so there's nothing else to do.
260: 		return nil
261: 	}
262: 	if _, ok := err.(*os.PathError); ok && lnType == "unix" {
263: 		// The underlying rmListener probably tried to remove
264: 		// the file but it didn't exist, ignore the error;
265: 		// this is a conflict between rmListener and the
266: 		// default Go behavior of removing auto-vivified
267: 		// Unix domain sockets.
268: 		return nil
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	return err
272: }

153: 		case "proxy":
154: 			if w.proxyListener == nil {
155: 				w.proxyListener = l
156: 			}
157: 			listenerCount++
158: 		}
159: 	}
160: 	if listenerCount != 1 {
161: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("exactly one proxy listener is required")
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	w.NodeeFileStorage, err = nodeefile.NewFileStorage(w.baseContext,
165: 		nodeefile.WithBaseDirectory(w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.AuthStoragePath))
166: 	if err != nil {
167: 		return nil, err
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	return w, nil
171: }
172: 
173: func (w *Worker) Start() error {
174: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).Start"
175: 
176: 	w.baseContext, w.baseCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
177: 
178: 	if w.started.Load() {
179: 		event.WriteSysEvent(w.baseContext, op, "already started, skipping")
180: 		return nil
181: 	}
182: 
183: 	scheme := strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 36)
184: 	controllerResolver := manual.NewBuilderWithScheme(scheme)
185: 	w.controllerResolver.Store(controllerResolver)
186: 
187: 	if err := w.startListeners(); err != nil {
188: 		return fmt.Errorf("error starting worker listeners: %w", err)
189: 	}
190: 
191: 	nodeCreds, err := types.NewNodeCredentials(w.baseContext, w.NodeeFileStorage, nodeenrollment.WithWrapper(w.conf.WorkerAuthStorageKms))
192: 	if err != nil {
193: 		return fmt.Errorf("error generating new node creds: %w", err)
194: 	}
195: 
196: 	req, err := nodeCreds.CreateFetchNodeCredentialsRequest(w.baseContext)
197: 	if err != nil {
198: 		return fmt.Errorf("error creating fetch credentials request: %w", err)
199: 	}
200: 	reqBytes, err := proto.Marshal(req)
201: 	if err != nil {
202: 		return fmt.Errorf("error marshaling fetch credentials request: %w", err)
203: 	}
204: 	w.NodeeRegistrationRequest = base58.FastBase58Encoding(reqBytes)
205: 	if err != nil {
206: 		return fmt.Errorf("error encoding registration request: %w", err)
207: 	}
208: 	w.NodeeCurrentKeyId, err = nodeenrollment.KeyIdFromPkix(nodeCreds.CertificatePublicKeyPkix)
209: 	if err != nil {
210: 		return fmt.Errorf("error deriving key id: %w", err)
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	if err := w.startControllerConnections(); err != nil {
214: 		return fmt.Errorf("error making controller connections: %w", err)
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	w.tickerWg.Add(1)
218: 	go func() {
219: 		defer w.tickerWg.Done()
220: 		w.startStatusTicking(w.baseContext)
221: 	}()
222: 
223: 	w.workerStartTime = time.Now()
224: 	w.started.Store(true)
225: 
226: 	return nil
227: }
228: 
229: // Shutdown shuts down the workers. skipListeners can be used to not stop
230: // listeners, useful for tests if we want to stop and start a worker. In order
231: // to create new listeners we'd have to migrate listener setup logic here --
232: // doable, but work for later.
233: func (w *Worker) Shutdown() error {
234: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).Shutdown"
235: 	if !w.started.Load() {
236: 		event.WriteSysEvent(w.baseContext, op, "already shut down, skipping")
237: 		return nil
238: 	}
239: 
240: 	// Stop listeners first to prevent new connections to the
241: 	// controller.
242: 	defer w.started.Store(false)
243: 	w.Resolver().UpdateState(resolver.State{Addresses: []resolver.Address{}})
244: 	w.baseCancel()
245: 
246: 	if err := w.stopServersAndListeners(); err != nil {
247: 		return fmt.Errorf("error stopping worker servers and listeners: %w", err)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	// Shut down all connections.
251: 	w.cleanupConnections(w.baseContext, true)
252: 
253: 	// Wait for next status request to succeed. Don't wait too long;
254: 	// wrap the base context in a timeout equal to our status grace
255: 	// period.
256: 	waitStatusStart := time.Now()
257: 	nextStatusCtx, nextStatusCancel := context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, w.conf.StatusGracePeriodDuration)
258: 	defer nextStatusCancel()
259: 	for {
260: 		if err := nextStatusCtx.Err(); err != nil {
261: 			event.WriteError(w.baseContext, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error waiting for next status report to controller"))
262: 			break
263: 		}
264: 
265: 		if w.lastSuccessfulStatusTime().Sub(waitStatusStart) > 0 {
266: 			break
267: 		}
268: 
269: 		time.Sleep(time.Second)
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	// Proceed with remainder of shutdown.
273: 	w.baseCancel()
274: 	w.Resolver().UpdateState(resolver.State{Addresses: []resolver.Address{}})
275: 
276: 	w.started.Store(false)
277: 	w.tickerWg.Wait()
278: 	if w.conf.Eventer != nil {
279: 		if err := w.conf.Eventer.FlushNodes(context.Background()); err != nil {
280: 			return fmt.Errorf("error flushing worker eventer nodes: %w", err)
281: 		}
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	return nil
285: }

287: func (w *Worker) Resolver() *manual.Resolver {
288: 	raw := w.controllerResolver.Load()
289: 	if raw == nil {
290: 		panic("nil resolver")
291: 	}
292: 	return raw.(*manual.Resolver)
293: }

295: func (w *Worker) ParseAndStoreTags(incoming map[string][]string) {
296: 	if len(incoming) == 0 {
297: 		w.tags.Store([]*servers.TagPair{})
298: 		return
299: 	}
300: 	tags := []*servers.TagPair{}
301: 	for k, vals := range incoming {
302: 		for _, v := range vals {
303: 			tags = append(tags, &servers.TagPair{
304: 				Key:   k,
305: 				Value: v,
306: 			})
307: 		}
308: 	}
309: 	w.tags.Store(tags)
310: 	w.updateTags.Store(true)
311: }

313: func (w *Worker) ControllerSessionConn() (pbs.SessionServiceClient, error) {
314: 	rawConn := w.controllerSessionConn.Load()
315: 	if rawConn == nil {
316: 		return nil, errors.New("unable to load controller session service connection")
317: 	}
318: 	sessClient, ok := rawConn.(pbs.SessionServiceClient)
319: 	if !ok {
320: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid session client %T", rawConn)
321: 	}
322: 	if sessClient == nil {
323: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session client is nil")
324: 	}
325: 
326: 	return sessClient, nil
327: }
328: 
329: func (w *Worker) getSessionTls(hello *tls.ClientHelloInfo) (*tls.Config, error) {
330: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).getSessionTls"
331: 	ctx := w.baseContext
332: 	var sessionId string
333: 	switch {
334: 	case strings.HasPrefix(hello.ServerName, globals.SessionPrefix):
335: 		sessionId = hello.ServerName
336: 	default:
337: 		for _, proto := range hello.SupportedProtos {
338: 			if strings.HasPrefix(proto, globals.SessionPrefix) {
339: 				sessionId = proto
340: 				break
341: 			}
342: 		}
343: 	}
344: 
345: 	if sessionId == "" {
346: 		event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "session_id not found in either SNI or ALPN protos", "server_name", hello.ServerName, "alpn_protos", hello.SupportedProtos)
347: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not find session ID in SNI or ALPN protos")
348: 	}
349: 
350: 	conn, err := w.ControllerSessionConn()
351: 	if err != nil {
352: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfo("failed to create controller session client"))
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	timeoutContext, cancel := context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, session.ValidateSessionTimeout)
356: 	defer cancel()
357: 
358: 	resp, err := conn.LookupSession(timeoutContext, &pbs.LookupSessionRequest{
359: 		ServerId:  w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Name,
360: 		SessionId: sessionId,
361: 	})
362: 	if err != nil {
363: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error validating session: %w", err)
364: 	}
365: 
366: 	if resp.GetExpiration().AsTime().Before(time.Now()) {
367: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session is expired")
368: 	}
369: 
370: 	parsedCert, err := x509.ParseCertificate(resp.GetAuthorization().Certificate)
371: 	if err != nil {
372: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing session certificate: %w", err)
373: 	}
374: 
375: 	certPool := x509.NewCertPool()
376: 	certPool.AddCert(parsedCert)
377: 
378: 	tlsConf := &tls.Config{
379: 		Certificates: []tls.Certificate{
380: 			{
381: 				Certificate: [][]byte{resp.GetAuthorization().Certificate},
382: 				PrivateKey:  ed25519.PrivateKey(resp.GetAuthorization().PrivateKey),
383: 				Leaf:        parsedCert,
384: 			},
385: 		},
386: 		NextProtos: []string{"http/1.1"},
387: 		MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS13,
388: 
389: 		// These two are set this way so we can make use of VerifyConnection,
390: 		// which we set on this TLS config below. We are not skipping
391: 		// verification!
392: 		ClientAuth:         tls.RequireAnyClientCert,
393: 		InsecureSkipVerify: true,
394: 	}
395: 
396: 	// We disable normal DNS SAN behavior as we don't rely on DNS or IP
397: 	// addresses for security and want to avoid issues with including localhost
398: 	// etc.
399: 	verifyOpts := x509.VerifyOptions{
400: 		DNSName: sessionId,
401: 		Roots:   certPool,
402: 		KeyUsages: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{
403: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth,
404: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth,
405: 		},
406: 	}
407: 	if w.TestOverrideX509VerifyCertPool != nil {
408: 		verifyOpts.Roots = w.TestOverrideX509VerifyCertPool
409: 	}
410: 	if w.TestOverrideX509VerifyDnsName != "" {
411: 		verifyOpts.DNSName = w.TestOverrideX509VerifyDnsName
412: 	}
413: 	tlsConf.VerifyConnection = func(cs tls.ConnectionState) error {
414: 		// Go will not run this without at least one peer certificate, but
415: 		// doesn't hurt to check
416: 		if len(cs.PeerCertificates) == 0 {
417: 			return errors.New("no peer certificates provided")
418: 		}
419: 		_, err := cs.PeerCertificates[0].Verify(verifyOpts)
420: 		return err
421: 	}
422: 
423: 	si := &session.Info{
424: 		Id:                    resp.GetAuthorization().GetSessionId(),
425: 		SessionTls:            tlsConf,
426: 		LookupSessionResponse: resp,
427: 		Status:                resp.GetStatus(),
428: 		ConnInfoMap:           make(map[string]*session.ConnInfo),
429: 	}
430: 	// TODO: Periodically clean this up. We can't rely on things in here but
431: 	// not in cancellation because they could be on the way to being
432: 	// established. However, since cert lifetimes are short, we can simply range
433: 	// through and remove values that are expired.
434: 	actualSiRaw, loaded := w.sessionInfoMap.LoadOrStore(sessionId, si)
435: 	if loaded {
436: 		// Update the response to the latest
437: 		actualSi := actualSiRaw.(*session.Info)
438: 		actualSi.Lock()
439: 		actualSi.LookupSessionResponse = resp
440: 		actualSi.Unlock()
441: 	}
442: 
443: 	return tlsConf, nil
444: }

36: func (w *Worker) startControllerConnections() error {
37: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).startControllerConnections"

39: 	for _, addr := range w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Controllers {

45: 			if err != nil && strings.Contains(err.Error(), "missing port in address") {
46: 				host, port, err = net.SplitHostPort(net.JoinHostPort(addr, "9201"))
47: 			}
48: 			if err != nil {
49: 				return fmt.Errorf("error parsing controller address: %w", err)
50: 			}
51: 			initialAddrs = append(initialAddrs, resolver.Address{Addr: net.JoinHostPort(host, port)})
52: 		}
53: 	}
54:

56: 		return errors.New("no initial controller addresses found")
57: 	}

69: func (w *Worker) controllerDialerFunc() func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {
70: 	return func(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
71: 		var conn net.Conn
72: 		var err error
73: 		switch {
74: 		case w.conf.WorkerAuthKms != nil:
75: 			conn, err = w.v1KmsAuthDialFn(ctx, addr)
76: 		default:
77: 			conn, err = protocol.Dial(ctx, w.NodeeFileStorage, addr, nodeenrollment.WithWrapper(w.conf.WorkerAuthStorageKms))
78: 		}
79: 
80: 		if !w.everAuthenticated.Load() && err == nil && conn != nil {
81: 			w.everAuthenticated.Store(true)
82: 		}
83: 
84: 		return conn, err
85: 	}
86: }

88: func (w *Worker) v1KmsAuthDialFn(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
89: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).v1KmsAuthDialFn"
90: 	tlsConf, authInfo, err := w.workerAuthTLSConfig()
91: 	if err != nil {
92: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating tls config for worker auth: %w", err)
93: 	}
94: 	dialer := &net.Dialer{}
95: 	var nonTlsConn net.Conn
96: 	switch {
97: 	case strings.HasPrefix(addr, "/"):
98: 		nonTlsConn, err = dialer.DialContext(ctx, "unix", addr)
99: 	default:
100: 		nonTlsConn, err = dialer.DialContext(ctx, "tcp", addr)
101: 	}
102: 	if err != nil {
103: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to dial to controller: %w", err)
104: 	}
105: 	tlsConn := tls.Client(nonTlsConn, tlsConf)
106: 	written, err := tlsConn.Write([]byte(authInfo.ConnectionNonce))
107: 	if err != nil {
108: 		if err := nonTlsConn.Close(); err != nil {
109: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error closing connection after writing failure"))
110: 		}
111: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to write connection nonce: %w", err)
112: 	}
113: 	if written != len(authInfo.ConnectionNonce) {
114: 		if err := nonTlsConn.Close(); err != nil {
115: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error closing connection after writing failure"))
116: 		}
117: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("expected to write %d bytes of connection nonce, wrote %d", len(authInfo.ConnectionNonce), written)
118: 	}
119: 	return tlsConn, nil
120: }

172: func (w *Worker) workerAuthTLSConfig() (*tls.Config, *base.WorkerAuthInfo, error) {

185: 	if err != nil {

187: 	}
188: 	host, err := base62.Random(20)
189: 	if err != nil {
190: 		return nil, nil, err
191: 	}
192: 
193: 	template := &x509.Certificate{
194: 		ExtKeyUsage: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{
195: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth,
196: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth,
197: 		},
198: 		DNSNames:              []string{host},
199: 		KeyUsage:              x509.KeyUsageDigitalSignature | x509.KeyUsageKeyEncipherment | x509.KeyUsageKeyAgreement | x509.KeyUsageCertSign,
200: 		SerialNumber:          big.NewInt(mathrand.Int63()),
201: 		NotBefore:             time.Now().Add(-30 * time.Second),
202: 		NotAfter:              time.Now().Add(globals.WorkerAuthNonceValidityPeriod),
203: 		BasicConstraintsValid: true,
204: 		IsCA:                  true,
205: 	}
206: 	certBytes, err := x509.CreateCertificate(w.conf.SecureRandomReader, template, template, pubKey, privKey)
207: 	if err != nil {
208: 		return nil, nil, err
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	certPEMBlock := &pem.Block{
212: 		Type:  "CERTIFICATE",
213: 		Bytes: certBytes,
214: 	}
215: 	info.CertPEM = pem.EncodeToMemory(certPEMBlock)
216: 
217: 	marshaledKey, err := x509.MarshalPKCS8PrivateKey(privKey)
218: 	if err != nil {
219: 		return nil, nil, err
220: 	}
221: 	keyPEMBlock := &pem.Block{
222: 		Type:  "PRIVATE KEY",
223: 		Bytes: marshaledKey,
224: 	}
225: 	info.KeyPEM = pem.EncodeToMemory(keyPEMBlock)
226: 
227: 	// Marshal and encrypt
228: 	marshaledInfo, err := json.Marshal(info)
229: 	if err != nil {
230: 		return nil, nil, err
231: 	}
232: 	encInfo, err := w.conf.WorkerAuthKms.Encrypt(w.baseContext, marshaledInfo)
233: 	if err != nil {
234: 		return nil, nil, err
235: 	}
236: 	marshaledEncInfo, err := proto.Marshal(encInfo)
237: 	if err != nil {
238: 		return nil, nil, err
239: 	}
240: 	b64alpn := base64.RawStdEncoding.EncodeToString(marshaledEncInfo)
241: 	var nextProtos []string
242: 	var count int
243: 	for i := 0; i < len(b64alpn); i += 230 {
244: 		end := i + 230
245: 		if end > len(b64alpn) {
246: 			end = len(b64alpn)
247: 		}
248: 		nextProtos = append(nextProtos, fmt.Sprintf("v1workerauth-%02d-%s", count, b64alpn[i:end]))
249: 		count++
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	cert, err := x509.ParseCertificate(certBytes)
253: 	if err != nil {
254: 		return nil, nil, err
255: 	}
256: 
257: 	// Build local tls config
258: 	rootCAs := x509.NewCertPool()
259: 	rootCAs.AddCert(cert)
260: 	tlsCert, err := tls.X509KeyPair(info.CertPEM, info.KeyPEM)
261: 	if err != nil {
262: 		return nil, nil, err
263: 	}
264: 	tlsConfig := &tls.Config{
265: 		ServerName:   host,
266: 		Certificates: []tls.Certificate{tlsCert},
267: 		RootCAs:      rootCAs,
268: 		NextProtos:   nextProtos,
269: 		MinVersion:   tls.VersionTLS13,
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	return tlsConfig, info, nil
273: }

19: // AttachWorkerIdToState accepts a workerId and creates a struct for use with the Nodeenrollment lib
20: // This is intended for use in worker authorization; AuthorizeNode in the lib accepts the option WithState
21: // so that the workerId is passed through to storage and associated with a WorkerAuth record
22: func AttachWorkerIdToState(ctx context.Context, workerId string) (*structpb.Struct, error) {
23: 	const op = "servers.(Worker).AttachWorkerIdToState"
24: 	if workerId == "" {
25: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing workerId")
26: 	}
27: 
28: 	workerMap := &workerAuthWorkerId{WorkerId: workerId}
29: 	stateOpt := structs.Map(workerMap)
30: 	return structpb.NewStruct(stateOpt)
31: }
32: 
33: // A Worker is a server that provides an address which can be used to proxy
34: // session connections. It can be tagged with custom tags and is used when
35: // authorizing and establishing a session.  It is owned by a scope.
36: type Worker struct {
37: 	*store.Worker
38: 
39: 	apiTags    []*Tag `gorm:"-"`
40: 	configTags []*Tag `gorm:"-"`
41: }
42: 
43: // NewWorker returns a new Worker. Valid options are WithName, WithDescription
44: // WithAddress, and WithWorkerTags. All other options are ignored.  This does
45: // not set any of the worker reported values.
46: func NewWorker(scopeId string, opt ...Option) *Worker {
47: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
48: 	return &Worker{
49: 		Worker: &store.Worker{
50: 			ScopeId:     scopeId,
51: 			Name:        opts.withName,
52: 			Description: opts.withDescription,
53: 			Address:     opts.withAddress,
54: 		},
55: 		apiTags: opts.withWorkerTags,
56: 	}
57: }
58: 
59: // NewWorkerForStatus returns a new Worker usable for status updates.
60: // Valid options are WithName, WithAddress, and WithWorkerTags, all of which
61: // are assigned to the worker reported variations of these fields.
62: // All other options are ignored.
63: func NewWorkerForStatus(scopeId string, opt ...Option) *Worker {
64: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
65: 	return &Worker{
66: 		Worker: &store.Worker{
67: 			ScopeId:               scopeId,
68: 			WorkerReportedName:    opts.withName,
69: 			WorkerReportedAddress: opts.withAddress,
70: 		},
71: 		configTags: opts.withWorkerTags,
72: 	}
73: }
74: 
75: // allocWorker will allocate a Worker
76: func allocWorker() Worker {
77: 	return Worker{Worker: &store.Worker{}}
78: }
79: 
80: func (w *Worker) clone() *Worker {
81: 	if w == nil {
82: 		return nil
83: 	}
84: 	cw := proto.Clone(w.Worker)
85: 	cWorker := &Worker{
86: 		Worker: cw.(*store.Worker),
87: 	}
88: 	if w.apiTags != nil {
89: 		cWorker.apiTags = make([]*Tag, 0, len(w.apiTags))
90: 		for _, t := range w.apiTags {
91: 			cWorker.apiTags = append(cWorker.apiTags, &Tag{Key: t.Key, Value: t.Value})
92: 		}
93: 	}
94: 	if w.configTags != nil {
95: 		cWorker.configTags = make([]*Tag, 0, len(w.configTags))
96: 		for _, t := range w.configTags {
97: 			cWorker.configTags = append(cWorker.configTags, &Tag{Key: t.Key, Value: t.Value})
98: 		}
99: 	}
100: 	return cWorker
101: }
102: 
103: // CanonicalAddress returns the actual address boundary believes should be used
104: // to communicate with this worker.  This will be the worker resource's address
105: // unless it is not set in which case it will use address the worker provides
106: // in its connection status updates.  If neither is available, an empty string
107: // is returned.
108: func (w *Worker) CanonicalAddress() string {
109: 	if w.GetAddress() != "" {
110: 		return w.GetAddress()
111: 	}
112: 	return w.GetWorkerReportedAddress()
113: }
114: 
115: // CanonicalTags is the deduplicated set of tags contained on both the resource
116: // set over the API as well as the tags reported by the worker itself.
117: func (w *Worker) CanonicalTags() map[string][]string {
118: 	dedupedTags := make(map[Tag]struct{})
119: 	for _, t := range w.apiTags {
120: 		dedupedTags[*t] = struct{}{}
121: 	}
122: 	for _, t := range w.configTags {
123: 		dedupedTags[*t] = struct{}{}
124: 	}
125: 	tags := make(map[string][]string)
126: 	for t := range dedupedTags {
127: 		tags[t.Key] = append(tags[t.Key], t.Value)
128: 	}
129: 	return tags
130: }
131: 
132: func (w *Worker) GetApiTags() map[string][]string {
133: 	tags := make(map[string][]string)
134: 	for _, t := range w.apiTags {
135: 		tags[t.Key] = append(tags[t.Key], t.Value)
136: 	}
137: 	return tags
138: }
139: 
140: func (w *Worker) GetConfigTags() map[string][]string {
141: 	tags := make(map[string][]string)
142: 	for _, t := range w.configTags {
143: 		tags[t.Key] = append(tags[t.Key], t.Value)
144: 	}
145: 	return tags
146: }
147: 
148: // GetLastStatusTime contains the last time the worker has reported to the
149: // controller its connection status.  If the worker has never reported to a
150: // controller then nil is returned.
151: func (w *Worker) GetLastStatusTime() *timestamp.Timestamp {
152: 	if w == nil || w.Worker == nil || w.Worker.GetLastStatusTime().AsTime() == timestamp.NegativeInfinityTS {
153: 		return nil
154: 	}
155: 	return w.Worker.GetLastStatusTime()
156: }
157: 
158: // TableName overrides the table name used by Worker to `server_worker`
159: func (Worker) TableName() string {
160: 	return "server_worker"
161: }
162: 
163: // workerAggregate contains an aggregated view of the values associated with
164: // a single worker.
165: type workerAggregate struct {
166: 	PublicId              string `gorm:"primary_key"`
167: 	ScopeId               string
168: 	Name                  string
169: 	Description           string
170: 	CreateTime            *timestamp.Timestamp
171: 	UpdateTime            *timestamp.Timestamp
172: 	Address               string
173: 	Version               uint32
174: 	ApiTags               string
175: 	WorkerReportedName    string
176: 	WorkerReportedAddress string
177: 	LastStatusTime        *timestamp.Timestamp
178: 	WorkerConfigTags      string
179: }
180: 
181: func (a *workerAggregate) toWorker(ctx context.Context) (*Worker, error) {
182: 	const op = "servers.(workerAggregate).toWorker"
183: 	worker := &Worker{
184: 		Worker: &store.Worker{
185: 			PublicId:              a.PublicId,
186: 			Name:                  a.Name,
187: 			Description:           a.Description,
188: 			Address:               a.Address,
189: 			CreateTime:            a.CreateTime,
190: 			UpdateTime:            a.UpdateTime,
191: 			ScopeId:               a.ScopeId,
192: 			Version:               a.Version,
193: 			WorkerReportedAddress: a.WorkerReportedAddress,
194: 			WorkerReportedName:    a.WorkerReportedName,
195: 			LastStatusTime:        a.LastStatusTime,
196: 		},
197: 	}
198: 	tags, err := tagsFromAggregatedTagString(ctx, a.ApiTags)
199: 	if err != nil {
200: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error parsing config tag string"))
201: 	}
202: 	worker.apiTags = tags
203: 
204: 	tags, err = tagsFromAggregatedTagString(ctx, a.WorkerConfigTags)
205: 	if err != nil {
206: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error parsing config tag string"))
207: 	}
208: 	worker.configTags = tags
209: 
210: 	return worker, nil
211: }
212: 
213: // tagsForAggregatedTagString parses a deliminated string in the format returned
214: // by the database for the server_worker_aggregate view and returns []*Tag.
215: // The string is in the format of key1Yvalue1Zkey2Yvalue2Zkey3Yvalue3. Y and Z
216: // ares chosen for deliminators since tag keys and values are restricted from
217: // having capitalized letters in them.
218: func tagsFromAggregatedTagString(ctx context.Context, s string) ([]*Tag, error) {
219: 	if s == "" {
220: 		return nil, nil
221: 	}
222: 	const op = "servers.tagsFromAggregatedTagString"
223: 	const aggregateDelimiter = "Z"
224: 	const pairDelimiter = "Y"
225: 	var tags []*Tag
226: 	for _, kv := range strings.Split(s, aggregateDelimiter) {
227: 		res := strings.SplitN(kv, pairDelimiter, 3)
228: 		if len(res) != 2 {
229: 			return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.Internal, op, "invalid aggregated tag pairs")
230: 		}
231: 		tags = append(tags, &Tag{
232: 			Key:   res[0],
233: 			Value: res[1],
234: 		})
235: 	}
236: 	return tags, nil
237: }
238: 
239: func (a *workerAggregate) GetPublicId() string {
240: 	return a.PublicId
241: }
242: 
243: func (workerAggregate) TableName() string {
244: 	return "server_worker_aggregate"
245: }
